name: [TC-901] Load Returnable Lines for S2 and Verify
steps:
1. Go to /transactions/supplier-return
2. Select supplier: Punjab Steel Works (S2)
3. Wait for returnable lines to load
expected_result:
- TX-P3 lines appear:
  Line 1: P2-V1 (6mm), Original Qty 100, Max Returnable 100
  Line 2: P2-V2 (12mm), Original Qty 50, Max Returnable 50
- Lines are linked to TX-P3
- If lines do NOT load: CRITICAL BUG — log immediately
notes: This is a prerequisite check before TC-902

name: [TC-902] Post Supplier Return TX-SR1 (Partial, S2) and Verify
steps:
1. On supplier return form for S2:
   - Date: 2026-03-21
   - Notes: Defective 6mm rods returned
   - Line: TX-P3 Line 1 (P2-V1), Return Qty 10 (out of 100)
   - Return value = 10 × 50 = PKR 500
2. Save Draft → verify NO side effects
3. Post
4. Check /supplier/detail/[S2_ID]:
   - Balance = PKR 4,500 (5,000 - 500)
   - Ledger: new RETURN entry -500, running balance 4,500
   - Open Documents: TX-P3 remaining reduced to PKR 4,500
5. Check /products/detail/[P2_ID]:
   - P2-V1 stock = 70 (80 - 10)
   - P2-V2 unchanged = 50
   - Movements: SUPPLIER_RETURN_OUT entry, qty -10
6. Dashboard: Payables = PKR 4,500
7. /reports/inventory-valuation: P2-V1 qty 70, value PKR 3,500
expected_result:
- S2 balance: PKR 4,500
- P2-V1 stock: 70
- Movement: SUPPLIER_RETURN_OUT qty -10
- Dashboard payables: PKR 4,500
notes: RECORD TX-SR1 ID. Payment accounts must NOT change (returns don't affect cash).

name: [TC-903] Post Supplier Return TX-SR2 (S1, Creates Credit Balance)
steps:
1. Go to /transactions/supplier-return, select S1
2. Verify TX-P1 and TX-P2 lines appear (all still returnable)
3. Return: TX-P1 Line 2 (P1-V2 Medium), Qty 2, value = 2×1100 = PKR 2,200
4. Date: 2026-03-22 → Post
5. Check /supplier/detail/[S1_ID]:
   - Balance = -PKR 2,200 (was 0, return creates credit)
   - Verify UI shows credit balance clearly (not confusingly as positive debt)
6. Check /products/detail/[P1_ID]:
   - P1-V2 stock = 0 (was 2, returned 2)
   - Movement: SUPPLIER_RETURN_OUT qty -2
expected_result:
- S1 balance: -PKR 2,200 (credit — they owe us)
- P1-V2 stock: 0
- UI must clearly show negative balance direction
notes: RECORD TX-SR2 ID. If UI shows -2200 as +2200 (positive): HIGH severity bug.

name: [TC-904] Over-Return Validation (Supplier)
steps:
1. Go to /transactions/supplier-return, select S2
2. Select TX-P3 Line 1 (P2-V1), enter Return Qty 101 (original was 100)
3. Test: is 101 blocked?
4. Then enter Return Qty 95 (already returned 10, so max should be 90)
5. Test: is 95 blocked with message 'Only 90 remaining to return'?
expected_result:
- Qty 101: blocked (cannot return more than original)
- Qty 95: blocked (already returned 10, max is 90)
- Max returnable field shows 90, not 100
- If over-return allowed: CRITICAL BUG (creates phantom inventory)
notes: Do NOT post anything in this test case

name: [TC-1001] Load Returnable Lines for C1 and Verify
steps:
1. Go to /transactions/customer-return
2. Select customer: Ahmed Traders (C1)
3. Wait for returnable lines to load
expected_result:
- TX-S1 lines: P1-V1 (Qty 8, max 8), P1-V2 (Qty 3, max 3)
- TX-S2 lines: P1-V1 (Qty 5, max 5)
- If lines do NOT load: CRITICAL BUG

name: [TC-1002] Post Customer Return TX-CR1 (C1, Creates Credit Balance)
steps:
1. Customer return for C1:
   - Date: 2026-03-23
   - Line: TX-S1 Line 1 (P1-V1), Return Qty 3, value = 3×2000 = PKR 6,000
2. Save Draft → verify NO side effects (C1 still 0, P1-V1 stock still 17)
3. Post
4. Check /customer/detail/[C1_ID]:
   - Balance = -PKR 6,000 (we owe them a refund)
   - Ledger: new RETURN entry -6,000, running balance -6,000
5. Check /products/detail/[P1_ID]:
   - P1-V1 stock = 20 (17 + 3, goods came back)
   - Movement: CUSTOMER_RETURN_IN qty +3
6. Verify UI clearly shows -6,000 as a credit (we owe them)
7. /reports/inventory-valuation: P1-V1 qty 20, value at cost 20×1500 = PKR 30,000
expected_result:
- C1 balance: -PKR 6,000
- P1-V1 stock: 20 (increased — goods returned to us)
- Movement type: CUSTOMER_RETURN_IN
- Inventory valuation uses cost price (1500), not sale price (2000)
notes: RECORD TX-CR1 ID. Key: stock goes UP on customer return (goods came back to us).

name: [TC-1003] Post Customer Return TX-CR2 (C2)
steps:
1. Customer return for C2:
   - Date: 2026-03-24
   - Line: TX-S3 Line 1 (P2-V1), Return Qty 5, value = 5×100 = PKR 500
2. Post
3. C2 balance = -PKR 500
4. P2-V1 stock = 75 (70 + 5)

name: [TC-1004] Over-Return and Re-Return Validation (Customer)
steps:
1. Customer return for C2, TX-S3 Line 1 (P2-V1 original qty 20): enter Qty 21
   Expected: blocked — cannot return more than sold
2. Customer return for C1, TX-S1 Line 1 (P1-V1 original 8, already returned 3):
   Enter Qty 6. Expected: blocked — max returnable is 5
3. Verify max returnable shows 5 (not 8)
expected_result:
- Qty 21 from a sale of 20: blocked
- Qty 6 when 3 already returned (max 5): blocked
- Max returnable reduces by already-returned amount

name: [TC-1005] Return Lifecycle — Cannot Delete Posted, Can Delete Draft
steps:
1. Open TX-SR1 (posted supplier return): verify Delete button absent or disabled
2. Create a new supplier return draft (any supplier, small qty)
3. Do NOT post it
4. Delete the draft
5. Verify draft gone from list, no stock/balance changes
expected_result:
- Posted return: cannot be deleted
- Draft return: can be deleted with no side effects
